content.full_text:
December 15, 1977


Honorable Robert K. Puglia
Presiding Justice
Court of Appeal
Third Appellate District
State Library and Courts Building
Sacramento, CA 95814

Dear Justice Puglia:

     At its December 7, 1977, meeting the Fair Political
Practices Commission considered the question of whether gifts
of food, travel and lodging made to the spouse of an official
in connection with the official's participation in a seminar
or panel are reportable on the official's Statement of Economic
Interests. The Commission decided that such gifts to the
official's spouse are not reportable by the official.

     As I believe you are aware, Commission regulations
(2 Cal. Adm. Code Sect 18728) exempt from reporting food,
travel and lodging provided to an official in connection with
his or her participation in or speaking at an in-state event,
seminar or panel. The regulation does not exempt from report-
ing food, travel and lodging provided to an official's spouse
who accompanies him or her to such an event. However, based
upon the Cory opinion (Opinion Requested by Ken Cory, 2 Appd.
Opinions 48, No. 75-094-A, April 22, 1976) which nerally
exempts from reporting gifts made to an official's spouse and
the policy embodied in section 18728, that an official is
unlikely to be improperly influenced by food, travel and
lodging provided in connection with panels and seminars in
which they participate, the Commission decided that the pro-
vision of food, travel and lodging to an accompanying spouse
should not be reportable.

     The Commission's decision is contrary to previous
advice the Commission staff has provided to you. It is my
understanding that you have amended your Statement of Economic
Interests to include gifts to your spouse which under the

Honorable Robert K. Puglia
December 15, 1977
Page Two


Commission's interpretation are not reportable. Therefore,
you may, if you desire, amend your statements to delete the
reporting of the gifts to your wife.

We apologize for the inconvenience we have caused
you by altering our advice and thank you for your interest
which brought a difficult issue to our attention. If you
have any further questions, please do not hesitate to call
me.

Sincerely,


Lee C. Rosenthal
Acting Chief
Legal Division

LCR:plh

State of California

Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. BOX 807 • SACRAMENTO, 95804 • • • 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814

Technical Assistance and Compliance ... Executive Legal ... Enforcement ... Conflict of Interest
(916) 322-5660 ... 322-5901 ... 322-6441 ... 322-6444

October 24, 1977


Justice Robert K. Puglia
Court of Appeal
Third Appellate District
Library and Courts Building
Sacramento, CA 95814

Dear Justice Puglia:

     In response to a letter from you dated August 8, 1977,
we responded on August 31, 1977, that you are required to report
on your Statement of Economic Interests the receipt by your wife
of food and accommodations at a workshop sponsored by the Cali-
fornia Academy of Appellate Lawyers in June, 1976. Recently,
the staff has had occasion to reassess its interpretation of the
reporting requirements which arises when an official's spouse is
the recipient of a gift. The question will be submitted to the
Commission at its next meeting in November, and it may be that
the final resolution of the matter will be not to require report-
ing of benefits of the type received by your wife at the Academy
workshop.

     Since you originally inquired about this problem, we
wanted to advise you of this development and welcome any comments
you may have prior to the Commission's review of this question.

                                        Sincerely,

                                        Michael J. Baker
                                        Chief
                                        Legal Division

MJB:jo
